data.selected_code:
def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_reg

summary.high_unstructured: The scrape_season_stats function downloads NBA per-game statistics for a given year from the Basketball Reference website. It reads the HTML table, removes any repeated header rows that may appear within the data, and adds a 'Year' column to the DataFrame. The function then filters the DataFrame to retain only the Player, Year, PTS, AST, TRB, and FG% columns, ensuring that all rows have valid values for these statistics by dropping any with missing data. Finally, it returns the cleaned and filtered DataFrame for further use.

data.updated_code:
def scrape_advanced_stats(year):
    base_url_adv = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_advanced.html"
    )
    df_adv = pd.read_html(base_url_adv, header=0)[0]
    df_adv = df_adv[df_adv["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_adv["Year"] = year

    # Keep only specified columns
    df_adv = df_adv[
        ["Player", "Year", "BPM", "WS/48", "WS", "PER", "TS%", "WORP"]
    ]
    df_adv = df_adv.dropna(
        subset=["BPM", "WS/48", "WS", "PER", "TS%", "WORP"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_adv

    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

    df_reg = df_reg[
        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_reg

metadata.originalCode:
def scrape_advanced_stats(year):
    base_url_adv = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_advanced.html"
    )
    df_adv = pd.read_html(base_url_adv, header=0)[0]
    df_adv = df_adv[df_adv["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_adv["Year"] = year

    # Keep only specified columns
    df_adv = df_adv[
        ["Player", "Year", "BPM", "WS/48", "WS", "PER", "TS%", "WORP"]
    ]
    df_adv = df_adv.dropna(
        subset=["BPM", "WS/48", "WS", "PER", "TS%", "WORP"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_adv

def scrape_season_stats(year):
    base_url_reg = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_per_game.html"
    )
    df_reg = pd.read_html(base_url_reg, header=0)[0]
    df_reg = df_reg[df_reg["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_reg["Year"] = year

        ["Player", "Year", "PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    ]  # Keep only specified columns
    df_reg = df_reg.dropna(
        subset=["PTS", "AST", "TRB", "FG%"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_reg

metadata.originalCode:
def scrape_season_advanced_stat(year):
    base_url_adv = (
        f"https://www.basketball-reference.com/leagues/NBA_{year}_advanced.html"
    )
    df_adv = pd.read_html(base_url_adv, header=0)[0]
    df_adv = df_adv[df_adv["Player"] != "Player"]  # remove repeated header rows
    df_adv["Year"] = year

    # Select only the required advanced stats columns
    df_adv = df_adv[
        ["Player", "Year", "BPM", "WS/48", "WS", "PER", "TS%", "WORP"]
    ]
    df_adv = df_adv.dropna(
        subset=["BPM", "WS/48", "WS", "PER", "TS%", "WORP"]
    )  # Ensure key stats aren't invalid
    return df_adv

    stats_df = scrape_multiple_seasons()
    stats_df.to_csv("data/season_stats.csv", index=False)

data.selected_code:
import pandas as pd
from xgboost import XGBRanker
from sklearn.metrics import ndcg_score
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns


def load_data(path="data/merged_data.csv"):
    df = pd.read_csv(path)

    # Features to use
    feature_cols = [
        "PTS",
        "AST",
        "TRB",
        "FG%",
        "BPM",
        "WS/48",
        "TS%",
        "VORP",
        "WS",
        "PER",
    ]
    feature_cols = [
        col for col in feature_cols if col in df.columns
    ]  # only keep existing features
    df = df.dropna(subset=feature_cols + ["Rank"])

    # Sort by Year and Rank for consistency
    df = df.sort_values(by=["Year", "Rank"])

    X = df[feature_cols]
    y = df["Rank"]
    groups = df.groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    return X, y, groups, df


def evaluate_ndcg(y_true, y_pred, group_sizes):
    scores = []
    start = 0
    for size in group_sizes:
        true_ranks = y_true[start : start + size]
        true_relevance = (max(true_ranks) + 1) - true_ranks
        pred_scores = y_pred[start : start + size]
        scores.append(ndcg_score([true_relevance], [pred_scores]))
        start += size
    return np.mean(scores)


def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")


def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Model
    model = XGBRanker(
        objective="rank:pairwise",
        learning_rate=0.1,
        n_estimators=100,
        max_depth=4,
        random_state=42,
    )

    print("Training XGBoostRanker...")
    model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
    print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

summary.high_unstructured: This code implements a pipeline for ranking basketball MVP candidates using an XGBoost ranking model. It begins by loading and cleaning the data, selecting only relevant statistical features and ensuring no missing values in the selected columns. The data is split into training and testing sets based on years, and group sizes are determined for proper ranking evaluation. The model is trained and used to predict ranks for the test years, with performance assessed using the NDCG metric. Finally, the script visualizes prediction errors for the latest years, saving bar plots that compare predicted and true ranks for each candidate.

60:     df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
61:     year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
62:     year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)
63: 
64:     plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
65:     sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
66:     plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
67:     plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
68:     plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
69:     plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
70:     plt.tight_layout()
71:     plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")
72: 
73: 
74: def main():
75:     X, y, group, df = load_data()
76: 
77:     # Train-test split on years
78:     unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
79:     train_years, test_years = (
80:         unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
81:         unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
82:     )
83: 
84:     train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
85:     test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)
86: 
87:     X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
88:     y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]
89: 
90:     # Group sizes
91:     train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
92:     test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
93: 
94:     # Model
95:     model = XGBRanker(
96:         objective="rank:pairwise",
97:         learning_rate=0.1,
98:         n_estimators=100,
99:         max_depth=4,
100:         random_state=42,
101:     )
102: 
103:     print("Training XGBoostRanker...")
104:     model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)
105: 
106:     # Predict, sort, and evaluate
107:     y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
108:     df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
109:     df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
110:         df.loc[test_idx]
111:         .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
112:         .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
113:     )
114: 
115:     ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
116:     print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")
117: 
118:     # Show results
119:     print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
120:     plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
121:     plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)
122: 
123: 
124: if __name__ == "__main__":
125:     main()

1: import pandas as pd
2: from xgboost import XGBRanker
3: from sklearn.metrics import ndcg_score
4: import numpy as np
5: import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
6: import seaborn as sns
7: 
8: 
9: def load_data(path="data/merged_data.csv"):
10:     df = pd.read_csv(path)
11: 
12:     # Features to use
13:     feature_cols = [
14:         "PTS",
15:         "AST",
16:         "TRB",
17:         "FG%",
18:         "BPM",
19:         "WS/48",
20:         "TS%",
21:         "VORP",
22:         "WS",
23:         "PER",
24:     ]
25:     feature_cols = [
26:         col for col in feature_cols if col in df.columns
27:     ]  # only keep existing features
28:     df = df.dropna(subset=feature_cols + ["Rank"])
29: 
30:     # Sort by Year and Rank for consistency
31:     df = df.sort_values(by=["Year", "Rank"])
32: 
33:     X = df[feature_cols]
34:     y = df["Rank"]
35:     groups = df.groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
36: 
37:     return X, y, groups, df
38: 
39: 
40: def evaluate_ndcg(y_true, y_pred, group_sizes):
41:     scores = []
42:     start = 0
43:     for size in group_sizes:
44:         true_ranks = y_true[start : start + size]
45:         true_relevance = (max(true_ranks) + 1) - true_ranks
46:         pred_scores = y_pred[start : start + size]
47:         scores.append(ndcg_score([true_relevance], [pred_scores]))
48:         start += size
49:     return np.mean(scores)
50: 
51:

53:     """
54:     Plot prediction error for a specific year.
55: 
56:     Args:

59:     """
60:     df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
61:     year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
62:     year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)
63: 
64:     plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
65:     sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
66:     plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
67:     plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
68:     plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
69:     plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
70:     plt.tight_layout()
71:     plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")
72: 
73: 
74: def main():
75:     X, y, group, df = load_data()
76: 
77:     # Train-test split on years
78:     unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
79:     train_years, test_years = (
80:         unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
81:         unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
82:     )
83: 
84:     train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
85:     test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)
86: 
87:     X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
88:     y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]
89: 
90:     # Group sizes
91:     train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
92:     test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
93: 
94:     # Model
95:     model = XGBRanker(
96:         objective="rank:pairwise",
97:         learning_rate=0.1,
98:         n_estimators=100,
99:         max_depth=4,
100:         random_state=42,
101:     )
102: 
103:     print("Training XGBoostRanker...")
104:     model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)
105: 
106:     # Predict, sort, and evaluate
107:     y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
108:     df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
109:     df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
110:         df.loc[test_idx]
111:         .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
112:         .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
113:     )
114: 
115:     ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
116:     print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")
117: 
118:     # Show results
119:     print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
120:     plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
121:     plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)
122: 
123: 
124: if __name__ == "__main__":
125:     main()

metadata.originalCode:
import pandas as pd
from xgboost import XGBRanker
from sklearn.metrics import ndcg_score
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns


def load_data(path="data/merged_data.csv"):
    df = pd.read_csv(path)

    # Features to use
    feature_cols = [
        "PTS",
        "AST",
        "TRB",
        "FG%",
        "BPM",
        "WS/48",
        "TS%",
        "VORP",
        "WS",
        "PER",
    ]
    feature_cols = [
        col for col in feature_cols if col in df.columns
    ]  # only keep existing features
    df = df.dropna(subset=feature_cols + ["Rank"])

    # Sort by Year and Rank for consistency
    df = df.sort_values(by=["Year", "Rank"])

    X = df[feature_cols]
    y = df["Rank"]
    groups = df.groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    return X, y, groups, df


def evaluate_ndcg(y_true, y_pred, group_sizes):
    scores = []
    start = 0
    for size in group_sizes:
        true_ranks = y_true[start : start + size]
        true_relevance = (max(true_ranks) + 1) - true_ranks
        pred_scores = y_pred[start : start + size]
        scores.append(ndcg_score([true_relevance], [pred_scores]))
        start += size
    return np.mean(scores)


def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")


def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Model
    model = XGBRanker(
        objective="rank:pairwise",
        learning_rate=0.1,
        n_estimators=100,
        max_depth=4,
        random_state=42,
    )

    print("Training XGBoostRanker...")
    model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
    print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import pandas as pd
from xgboost import XGBRanker
from sklearn.metrics import ndcg_score
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns


def load_data(path="data/merged_data.csv"):
    df = pd.read_csv(path)

    # Features to use
    feature_cols = [
        "PTS",
        "AST",
        "TRB",
        "FG%",
        "BPM",
        "WS/48",
        "TS%",
        "VORP",
        "WS",
        "PER",
    ]
    feature_cols = [
        col for col in feature_cols if col in df.columns
    ]  # only keep existing features
    df = df.dropna(subset=feature_cols + ["Rank"])

    # Sort by Year and Rank for consistency
    df = df.sort_values(by=["Year", "Rank"])

    X = df[feature_cols]
    y = df["Rank"]
    groups = df.groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    return X, y, groups, df


def evaluate_ndcg(y_true, y_pred, group_sizes):
    scores = []
    start = 0
    for size in group_sizes:
        true_ranks = y_true[start : start + size]
        true_relevance = (max(true_ranks) + 1) - true_ranks
        pred_scores = y_pred[start : start + size]
        scores.append(ndcg_score([true_relevance], [pred_scores]))
        start += size
    return np.mean(scores)


def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")


def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    ndcg_results = {}
    for n_estimators in [100, 1000, 2000]:
        model = XGBRanker(
            objective="rank:pairwise",
            learning_rate=0.1,
            n_estimators=n_estimators,
            max_depth=4,
            random_state=42,
        )

        print(f"Training XGBoostRanker with n_estimators={n_estimators}...")
        model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

        # Predict, sort, and evaluate
        y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
        df.loc[test_idx, f"PredictedScore_{n_estimators}"] = y_pred
        df.loc[test_idx, f"PredictedRank_{n_estimators}"] = (
            df.loc[test_idx]
            .groupby("Year")[f"PredictedScore_{n_estimators}"]
            .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
        )

        ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
        ndcg_results[n_estimators] = ndcg
        print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()} with n_estimators={n_estimators}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Use the results from the last n_estimators (2000) for visualization and output
    last_n = 2000
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", f"PredictedScore_{last_n}", f"PredictedRank_{last_n}"]])
    df["PredictedRank"] = df.get(f"PredictedRank_{last_n}", np.nan)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

from xgboost import XGBRanker
from sklearn.metrics import ndcg_score
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns


def load_data(path="data/merged_data.csv"):
    df = pd.read_csv(path)

    # Features to use
    feature_cols = [
        "PTS",
        "AST",
        "TRB",
        "FG%",
        "BPM",
        "WS/48",
        "TS%",
        "VORP",
        "WS",
        "PER",
    ]
    feature_cols = [
        col for col in feature_cols if col in df.columns
    ]  # only keep existing features
    df = df.dropna(subset=feature_cols + ["Rank"])

    # Sort by Year and Rank for consistency
    df = df.sort_values(by=["Year", "Rank"])

    X = df[feature_cols]
    y = df["Rank"]
    groups = df.groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    return X, y, groups, df


def evaluate_ndcg(y_true, y_pred, group_sizes):
    scores = []
    start = 0
    for size in group_sizes:
        true_ranks = y_true[start : start + size]
        true_relevance = (max(true_ranks) + 1) - true_ranks
        pred_scores = y_pred[start : start + size]
        scores.append(ndcg_score([true_relevance], [pred_scores]))
        start += size
    return np.mean(scores)


def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")


def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    # Model
    model = XGBRanker(
        objective="rank:pairwise",
        learning_rate=0.1,
        n_estimators=100,
        max_depth=4,
        random_state=42,
    )

    print("Training XGBoostRanker...")
    model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

    # Predict, sort, and evaluate
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedScore"] = y_pred
    df.loc[test_idx, "PredictedRank"] = (
        df.loc[test_idx]
        .groupby("Year")["PredictedScore"]
        .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
    )

    ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
    print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Show results
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", "PredictedScore", "PredictedRank"]])
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import pandas as pd
from xgboost import XGBRanker
from sklearn.metrics import ndcg_score
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns


def load_data(path="data/merged_data.csv"):
    df = pd.read_csv(path)

    # Features to use
    feature_cols = [
        "PTS",
        "AST",
        "TRB",
        "FG%",
        "BPM",
        "WS/48",
        "TS%",
        "VORP",
        "WS",
        "PER",
    ]
    feature_cols = [
        col for col in feature_cols if col in df.columns
    ]  # only keep existing features
    df = df.dropna(subset=feature_cols + ["Rank"])

    # Sort by Year and Rank for consistency
    df = df.sort_values(by=["Year", "Rank"])

    y = df["Rank"]
    groups = df.groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    return X, y, groups, df


def evaluate_ndcg(y_true, y_pred, group_sizes):
    scores = []
    start = 0
    for size in group_sizes:
        true_ranks = y_true[start : start + size]
        true_relevance = (max(true_ranks) + 1) - true_ranks
        pred_scores = y_pred[start : start + size]
        scores.append(ndcg_score([true_relevance], [pred_scores]))
        start += size
    return np.mean(scores)


def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")


def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    ndcg_results = {}
    for n_estimators in [100, 1000, 2000]:
        model = XGBRanker(
            objective="rank:pairwise",
            learning_rate=0.1,
            n_estimators=n_estimators,
            max_depth=4,
            random_state=42,
        )

        print(f"Training XGBoostRanker with n_estimators={n_estimators}...")
        model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

        # Predict, sort, and evaluate

            .groupby("Year")[f"PredictedScore_{n_estimators}"]
            .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
        )

        ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
        ndcg_results[n_estimators] = ndcg
        print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()} with n_estimators={n_estimators}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Use the results from the last n_estimators (2000) for visualization and output
    last_n = 2000
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", f"PredictedScore_{last_n}", f"PredictedRank_{last_n}"]])
    df["PredictedRank"] = df.get(f"PredictedRank_{last_n}", np.nan)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

For each n_estimators value (100, 1000, 2000), the model is trained and used to predict ranks for the test years, with performance assessed using the NDCG metric and results stored for each configuration. Finally, the script visualizes prediction errors for the latest years, saving bar plots that compare predicted and true ranks for each candidate using the predictions from the model with 2000 estimators.

data.code:
1: import pandas as pd
2: from xgboost import XGBRanker
3: from sklearn.metrics import ndcg_score
4: import numpy as np
5: import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
6: import seaborn as sns
7: 
8: 
9: def load_data(path="data/merged_data.csv"):
10:     df = pd.read_csv(path)
11: 
12:     # Features to use
13:     feature_cols = [
14:         "PTS",
15:         "AST",
16:         "TRB",
17:         "FG%",
18:         "BPM",
19:         "WS/48",
20:         "TS%",
21:         "VORP",
22:         "WS",
23:         "PER",
24:     ]
25:     feature_cols = [
26:         col for col in feature_cols if col in df.columns
27:     ]  # only keep existing features
28:     df = df.dropna(subset=feature_cols + ["Rank"])
29: 
30:     # Sort by Year and Rank for consistency
31:     df = df.sort_values(by=["Year", "Rank"])
32: 
33:     X = df[feature_cols]
34:     y = df["Rank"]
35:     groups = df.groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
36: 
37:     return X, y, groups, df
38: 
39: 
40: def evaluate_ndcg(y_true, y_pred, group_sizes):
41:     scores = []
42:     start = 0
43:     for size in group_sizes:
44:         true_ranks = y_true[start : start + size]
45:         true_relevance = (max(true_ranks) + 1) - true_ranks
46:         pred_scores = y_pred[start : start + size]
47:         scores.append(ndcg_score([true_relevance], [pred_scores]))
48:         start += size
49:     return np.mean(scores)
50: 
51: 
52: def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
53:     """
54:     Plot prediction error for a specific year.
55: 
56:     Args:
57:         df: DataFrame containing prediction results
58:         year: Year to plot prediction errors for
59:     """
60:     df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
61:     year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
62:     year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)
63: 
64:     plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
65:     sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
66:     plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
67:     plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
68:     plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
69:     plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
70:     plt.tight_layout()
71:     plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")
72: 
73: 
74: def main():
75:     X, y, group, df = load_data()
76: 
77:     # Train-test split on years
78:     unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
79:     train_years, test_years = (
80:         unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
81:         unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
82:     )
83: 
84:     train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
85:     test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)
86: 
87:     X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
88:     y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]
89: 
90:     # Group sizes
91:     train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
92:     test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
93: 
94:     ndcg_results = {}
95:     for n_estimators in [100, 1000, 2000]:
96:         model = XGBRanker(
97:             objective="rank:pairwise",
98:             learning_rate=0.1,
99:             n_estimators=n_estimators,
100:             max_depth=4,
101:             random_state=42,
102:         )
103: 
104:         print(f"Training XGBoostRanker with n_estimators={n_estimators}...")
105:         model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)
106: 
107:         # Predict, sort, and evaluate
108:         y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
109:         df.loc[test_idx, f"PredictedScore_{n_estimators}"] = y_pred
110:         df.loc[test_idx, f"PredictedRank_{n_estimators}"] = (
111:             df.loc[test_idx]
112:             .groupby("Year")[f"PredictedScore_{n_estimators}"]
113:             .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
114:         )
115: 
116:         ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
117:         ndcg_results[n_estimators] = ndcg
118:         print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()} with n_estimators={n_estimators}: {ndcg:.4f}")
119: 
120:     # Use the results from the last n_estimators (2000) for visualization and output
121:     last_n = 2000
122:     print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", f"PredictedScore_{last_n}", f"PredictedRank_{last_n}"]])
123:     df["PredictedRank"] = df.get(f"PredictedRank_{last_n}", np.nan)
124:     plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
125:     plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)
126: 
127: 
128: if __name__ == "__main__":
129:     main()

100:             max_depth=4,
101:             random_state=42,
102:         )
103: 
104:         print(f"Training XGBoostRanker with n_estimators={n_estimators}...")
105:         model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)
106:

108:         y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
109:         df.loc[test_idx, f"PredictedScore_{n_estimators}"] = y_pred
110:         df.loc[test_idx, f"PredictedRank_{n_estimators}"] = (
111:             df.loc[test_idx]
112:             .groupby("Year")[f"PredictedScore_{n_estimators}"]
113:             .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
114:         )
115: 
116:         ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
117:         ndcg_results[n_estimators] = ndcg
118:         print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()} with n_estimators={n_estimators}: {ndcg:.4f}")

metadata.originalCode:
import pandas as pd
from xgboost import XGBRanker
from sklearn.metrics import ndcg_score
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns


def load_data(path="data/merged_data.csv"):
    df = pd.read_csv(path)

    # Features to use
    feature_cols = [
        "PTS",
        "AST",
        "TRB",
        "FG%",
        "BPM",
        "WS/48",
        "TS%",
        "VORP",
        "WS",
        "PER",
    ]
    feature_cols = [
        col for col in feature_cols if col in df.columns
    ]  # only keep existing features
    df = df.dropna(subset=feature_cols + ["Rank"])

    # Sort by Year and Rank for consistency
    df = df.sort_values(by=["Year", "Rank"])

    X = df[feature_cols]
    y = df["Rank"]
    groups = df.groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    return X, y, groups, df


def evaluate_ndcg(y_true, y_pred, group_sizes):
    scores = []
    start = 0
    for size in group_sizes:
        true_ranks = y_true[start : start + size]
        true_relevance = (max(true_ranks) + 1) - true_ranks
        pred_scores = y_pred[start : start + size]
        scores.append(ndcg_score([true_relevance], [pred_scores]))
        start += size
    return np.mean(scores)


def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")


def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    ndcg_results = {}
    for n_estimators in [100, 1000, 2000]:
        model = XGBRanker(
            objective="rank:pairwise",
            learning_rate=0.1,
            n_estimators=n_estimators,
            max_depth=4,
            random_state=42,
        )

        print(f"Training XGBoostRanker with n_estimators={n_estimators}...")
        model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

        # Predict, sort, and evaluate
        y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
        df.loc[test_idx, f"PredictedScore_{n_estimators}"] = y_pred
        df.loc[test_idx, f"PredictedRank_{n_estimators}"] = (
            df.loc[test_idx]
            .groupby("Year")[f"PredictedScore_{n_estimators}"]
            .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
        )

        ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
        ndcg_results[n_estimators] = ndcg
        print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()} with n_estimators={n_estimators}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Use the results from the last n_estimators (2000) for visualization and output
    last_n = 2000
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", f"PredictedScore_{last_n}", f"PredictedRank_{last_n}"]])
    df["PredictedRank"] = df.get(f"PredictedRank_{last_n}", np.nan)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

def main():
    X, y, group, df = load_data()

    # Train-test split on years
    unique_years = df["Year"].unique()
    train_years, test_years = (
        unique_years[:-2],  # train: 2015-2023
        unique_years[-2:],  # test: 2024-2025
    )

    train_idx = df["Year"].isin(train_years)
    test_idx = df["Year"].isin(test_years)

    X_train, X_test = X[train_idx], X[test_idx]
    y_train, y_test = y[train_idx], y[test_idx]

    # Group sizes
    train_groups = df[train_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()
    test_groups = df[test_idx].groupby("Year").size().to_numpy()

    ndcg_results = {}
    for n_estimators in [100, 1000, 2000]:
        model = XGBRanker(
            objective="rank:pairwise",
            learning_rate=0.1,
            n_estimators=n_estimators,
            max_depth=4,
            random_state=42,
        )

        print(f"Training XGBoostRanker with n_estimators={n_estimators}...")
        model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

        # Predict, sort, and evaluate
        y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
        df.loc[test_idx, f"PredictedScore_{n_estimators}"] = y_pred
        df.loc[test_idx, f"PredictedRank_{n_estimators}"] = (
            df.loc[test_idx]
            .groupby("Year")[f"PredictedScore_{n_estimators}"]
            .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
        )

        ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
        ndcg_results[n_estimators] = ndcg
        print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()} with n_estimators={n_estimators}: {ndcg:.4f}")

    # Use the results from the last n_estimators (2000) for visualization and output
    last_n = 2000
    print(df[test_idx][["Year", "Name", "Rank", f"PredictedScore_{last_n}", f"PredictedRank_{last_n}"]])
    df["PredictedRank"] = df.get(f"PredictedRank_{last_n}", np.nan)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2024)
    plot_ranking_predictions(df, 2025)

def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")

    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

            n_estimators=n_estimators,
            max_depth=4,
            random_state=42,
        )

        print(f"Training XGBoostRanker with n_estimators={n_estimators}...")
        model.fit(X_train, y_train, group=train_groups)

        # Predict, sort, and evaluate
        y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
        df.loc[test_idx, f"PredictedScore_{n_estimators}"] = y_pred
        df.loc[test_idx, f"PredictedRank_{n_estimators}"] = (
            df.loc[test_idx]
            .groupby("Year")[f"PredictedScore_{n_estimators}"]
            .rank(ascending=False, method="first")
        )

        ndcg = evaluate_ndcg(y_test.to_numpy(), y_pred, test_groups)
        ndcg_results[n_estimators] = ndcg
        print(f"NDCG Score on test years {test_years.tolist()} with n_estimators={n_estimators}: {ndcg:.4f}")

model = XGBRanker(
            objective="rank:pairwise",
            learning_rate=0.1,
            n_estimators=n_estimators,
            max_depth=4,
            random_state=42,
        )

high_structured.summaryComponent: Trains multiple XGBoost ranking models (XGBRanker) with varying n_estimators values (100, 1000, 2000)
For each model, predicts scores and computes predicted ranks for test years
Assigns predicted scores and ranks for each estimator setting back to the dataframe
Evaluates model performance for each n_estimators value using the NDCG metric, averaging scores across test groups
Visualizes prediction errors for the most recent years (2024 and 2025)
Creates and saves bar plots showing the difference between predicted and true ranks for each candidate using the results from the model with 2000 estimators

def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

The plot is formatted for readability and saved as a PNG file named according to the year.

def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot true rank and predicted rank for a specific year as a grouped bar chart.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    melted = year_df.melt(
        id_vars=["Name"],
        value_vars=["Rank", "PredictedRank"],
        var_name="Type",
        value_name="RankValue"
    )
    sns.barplot(
        x="Name",
        y="RankValue",
        hue="Type",
        data=melted,
        palette="coolwarm"
    )
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP True vs Predicted Rankings ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

data.original_code:
def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot prediction error for a specific year.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    df["RankError"] = df["PredictedRank"] - df["Rank"]
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    sns.barplot(x="Name", y="RankError", data=year_df, hue="Name", palette="coolwarm")
    plt.axhline(0, color="black", linestyle="--")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Predicted Rank - True Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP Ranking Predictions ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")

metadata.originalCode:
def plot_ranking_predictions(df, year):
    """
    Plot true rank and predicted rank for a specific year as a grouped bar chart.

    Args:
        df: DataFrame containing prediction results
        year: Year to plot prediction errors for
    """
    year_df = df[df["Year"] == year].copy()
    year_df.sort_values("Rank", inplace=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    melted = year_df.melt(
        id_vars=["Name"],
        value_vars=["Rank", "PredictedRank"],
        var_name="Type",
        value_name="RankValue"
    )
    sns.barplot(
        x="Name",
        y="RankValue",
        hue="Type",
        data=melted,
        palette="coolwarm"
    )
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Rank")
    plt.title(f"MVP True vs Predicted Rankings ({year})")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(f"data/ranking_predictions_{year}.png")
